<!-- Canonical runtime context lives in .agentweave/context/<agent>.md -->

# AgentWeave Bootstrap

You are in an AgentWeave project. This root file is only a bootstrap for tools
that auto-read `CLAUDE.md`.

## Load Your Runtime Context

1. Determine your AgentWeave agent name from the launch command, session, or prompt.
2. Read `.agentweave/context/<agent>.md` if it exists. That file is the canonical
   runtime context and includes your project operating profile, team, quality
   gates, role contracts, project instructions, and project context.
3. If the file is missing, run `agentweave sync-context --agent <agent>` or ask
   the principal to refresh context.

## Coordination

- If AgentWeave MCP tools are available, use them for coordination:
  `get_inbox`, `send_message`, `list_tasks`, `get_task`, `create_task`,
  `update_task`, `ask_user`, and `save_checkpoint`.
- In Hub/MCP mode, do not use `agentweave relay` or `agentweave quick` for
  delegation.
- For manual sessions, read `.agentweave/shared/context.md` before starting work
  if it exists.

## Fallback

If `.agentweave/context/<agent>.md` cannot be loaded, read only the minimum
needed files:

- `.agentweave/roles.json`
- your assigned files under `.agentweave/roles/`
- `.agentweave/protocol.md`
- `.agentweave/shared/context.md`
